digest.top_line: LBA8005: concurrent thoracic RT adds nothing to chemoIO in ES-SCLC (OS 10.0 vs 11.8mo, HR 1.14, p=0.40). First randomized TRT test in the IO era, and it's negative.
digest.tldr: The day's thread is RT plus immunotherapy integration, running in opposite directions. Thoracic: adding concurrent thoracic RT to chemoIO in ES-SCLC was flatly negative (LBA8005). Lower-GI: node-sparing short-course RT + tislelizumab doubled pCR in pMMR/MSS rectal (mRCAT-III, 61 vs 29%), though confounded. Breast adds a practical RT/systemic concurrency guide.

sites.intro: First randomized read on adding consolidative thoracic RT to modern chemoIO, and it lands null.

studies.name: Concurrent Thoracic RT + Chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC (LBA8005)
studies.tldr: Adding concurrent thoracic RT to chemoimmunotherapy did not improve OS (10.0 vs 11.8mo, HR 1.14, p=0.40) in extensive-stage SCLC.
details: 🔍 Randomized phase 3, N=228 (115 chemoIO+TRT vs 113 chemoIO); stratified by liver and brain mets
🔍 Thoracic RT 30 Gy/10 fx, concurrent, starting day 21-28 (cycle 2)
💊 Backbone: 4× durvalumab 1500mg + carboplatin AUC5 + etoposide, then durvalumab Q4W maintenance
🔗 vs CREST (Slotman, Lancet 2015): identical 30 Gy/10 fx improved 2yr OS in the pre-IO era
🔗 First randomized test of thoracic RT in the chemoIO era, and it is negative
⚠️ No rescue in the 'best' subgroups: chemo completers and pts without brain/liver mets both null
⚠️ TRT arm trended numerically worse for OS, not merely null; no directional hint of benefit
🔍 PCI and WBRT permitted in both arms, so thoracic RT is the only randomized variable

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase 3, prespecified OS primary not met (HR 1.14); diverges from CREST-era thoracic RT benefit at the identical 30 Gy/10 fx dose.
verdict.audience: Treatment-naive extensive-stage SCLC starting durvalumab + platinum/etoposide

studies.significance: First randomized test of thoracic RT in the chemoIO era, and it is negative: identical 30 Gy/10 fx to CREST but no OS benefit (HR 1.14) and null in every subgroup shown, including chemo completers. Stops the CREST extrapolation to durvalumab-treated ES-SCLC; concurrent thoracic RT is not supported here.
studies.monday_clinic: In first-line ES-SCLC on durvalumab plus platinum/etoposide, this questions adding concurrent thoracic RT (30 Gy/10 fx from cycle 2); it does not settle sequential consolidative thoracic RT given after chemoIO, the CREST timing.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Randomized phase 3, 1:1, N=228 (115 chemoIO+TRT vs 113 chemoIO), stratified by liver and brain metastases. **Primary endpoint: overall survival.**

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Treatment-naive ES-SCLC (stage IV, or III ineligible for curative chemoRT), ECOG 0-1, ≥1 measurable thoracic lesion. Asymptomatic/stable brain mets allowed.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: 4 cycles durvalumab 1500 mg + carboplatin AUC 5 + etoposide Q3W, then durvalumab 1500 mg Q4W maintenance until progression.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Concurrent thoracic RT **30 Gy in 10 fractions**, starting day 21-28 (cycle 2). PCI 25-30 Gy to responders and WBRT 20-30 Gy for brain mets permitted in both arms.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: Primary overall survival. Key secondary: ORR, PFS, toxicity.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **No OS or PFS benefit** from added thoracic RT; every prespecified subgroup shown was also null (see figures).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: CREST (Slotman, Lancet 2015) used the identical 30 Gy/10 fx thoracic RT and improved 2-yr OS in the pre-immunotherapy era; this is the first randomized test in the chemoIO era and is **negative**.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents treatment-naive extensive-stage SCLC starting durvalumab plus platinum/etoposide with measurable thoracic disease. Does not represent limited-stage SCLC or sequential post-chemo consolidative thoracic RT.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Concurrent timing (day 21-28) differs from CREST's post-chemo consolidation, so the sequential-timing question is **untouched**. TRT arm favored numerically worse across the subgroups shown.

open_questions: Does sequential consolidative thoracic RT after chemoIO benefit ES-SCLC?
Any ES-SCLC subgroup benefiting from thoracic RT in the IO era?

Based on the biomarker test results, study doctors can determine the subtype of ES-SCLC that study treatments can target. This study also tests different types of…

sites.intro: IO enters pMMR/MSS rectal, the setting where it usually does nothing, paired with a node-sparing RT change.

studies.name: mRCAT-III
studies.tldr: pCR 61.0 vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) with node-sparing SCRT + tislelizumab vs conventional SCRT, both + CAPOX, in pMMR/MSS LARC.
details: 🔍 Phase 3 open-label RCT, 17 China sites, N=154 (77/arm), stratified by cN (cN0 vs cN+)
📐 Both arms 5Gy×5 short-course RT; experimental spares tumor-draining nodes (tumor bed only)
💊 Both arms CAPOX; experimental adds tislelizumab 200mg IV D1 (anti-PD1)
⚠️ Confounded: experimental changes TWO things at once (node-sparing RT + tislelizumab); pCR gain not attributable to nodal omission alone
⚠️ pMMR/MSS setting where IO is usually inactive; the immune-sparing mechanism is hypothesis, not proven by this design
⚠️ 1° EP pCR is a surrogate; organ preservation, EFS, OS all secondary and immature
📊 Reported fewer severe GI AEs in experimental arm; no toxicity numbers given in source
🔍 Eligibility: cT3-4N0/+M0, tumor ≤10cm from anal verge, no positive lateral node, age 18-75, ECOG 0-1

verdict.rationale: Experimental arm alters two variables at once (node-sparing RT + tislelizumab), so the pCR gain can't be attributed to nodal omission; pCR surrogate, open-label, clinical endpoints immature.
verdict.audience: pMMR/MSS LARC, cT3-4N0/+, ≤10cm from anal verge, no lateral nodes

studies.significance: The RT read: node-sparing (tumor-bed-only 5Gy×5) and added tislelizumab move together in the experimental arm, so the doubled pCR (61.0 vs 28.6%) can't be attributed to nodal omission alone. It doesn't yet move the elective-nodal-coverage decision, a factorial or IO-matched design is needed before dropping nodal RT in pMMR LARC.
studies.monday_clinic: In pMMR/MSS distal LARC (cT3-4, tumor ≤10cm from verge, no lateral nodes), this is early evidence for adding tislelizumab to short-course RT; it does not extend to dMMR/MSI-H tumors, upper-rectal cancers, or lateral-node-positive disease.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Phase 3 open-label RCT**, 1:1, **N=154** across 17 China sites; stratified by clinical N stage (cN0 vs cN+). Primary endpoint **pCR** by blinded independent central review.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **pMMR/MSS** rectal adenocarcinoma, **cT3-4N0/+M0**, tumor lower edge **≤10cm from anal verge**, no positive lateral lymph node, age 18-75, ECOG 0-1.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Both arms CAPOX (oxaliplatin 130mg/m² D1, capecitabine 1000mg/m² D1-14). **Experimental adds tislelizumab 200mg D1**.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Both arms **5Gy×5 short-course RT**. Experimental uses **node-sparing** modified target (tumor bed only, sparing tumor-draining lymph nodes); control uses conventional short-course RT covering nodes.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** pCR (ITT). Secondary: MPR, TRG, organ preservation rate, EFS, OS, AEs.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: pCR **61.0 vs 28.6%** and MPR **77.9 vs 50.6%**, both P<0.0001 (see figure). Organ preservation, EFS, OS not yet reported.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents pMMR/MSS distal locally advanced rectal cancer (cT3-4, ≤10cm from verge, no lateral nodal disease). Does not represent dMMR/MSI-H tumors, upper-rectal cancers, or lateral-node-positive disease.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label, and **pCR is a surrogate** with immature clinical endpoints. The experimental arm alters two variables at once (node-sparing RT plus tislelizumab), so the gain **cannot be attributed to nodal omission**.

open_questions: Is the pCR gain from node-sparing RT or from added tislelizumab?
Does the pCR benefit translate to organ preservation, EFS, or OS?
Can elective nodal RT be safely omitted without raising nodal recurrence?

sites.intro: Not a trial, a practical map of which systemic agents run concurrent with locoregional RT.

studies.name: RT + Systemic Therapy Sequencing (Speers ASCO26 talk)
studies.tldr: Which breast systemic agents run concurrent with locoregional RT vs hold: endocrine + HER2 mAbs continue, cytotoxics/PARPi sequence, T-DXd + CDK4/6i case-by-case.

details.text: 🔍 Traffic-light guide: concurrent systemic therapy with breast / CW + RNI RT

rows: Continue during locoregional RT; CNS-SRS caution
Caution; sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active pulmonary disease
Reasonable with limited fields; concurrent only in protocol
Usually hold large fields; concurrent only in selected/protocol

details: 🔍 Speers ASCO26 synthesis (adapted from ASCO Educational Book 2026, Table 5): what to continue, hold, or sequence during breast/CW + nodal RT
⚠️ T-DXd dominant toxicity is ILD; PI (5.4 mg/kg) ILD ~12%, fatal ~0.9%
📊 DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1
📊 T-DXd RT timing showed no ILD effect: 10.7% sequential vs 9.6% concurrent
🔗 COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT with T-DXd, acute tox 20%
🔗 KEYNOTE-522 post-hoc (1,174 pts, 715 got RT): concurrent pembro + RT well tolerated, numerically fewer G3-5 and immune AEs vs sequential
📊 P-RAD (TBCRC-053): preop RT 0/9/24 Gy + pembro in HR+/HER2−; 24 Gy arm raised T-cell infiltration at 2 wks, induced tertiary lymphoid structures
⚠️ Olaparib: complete RT 2-12 wks before starting (sequential paradigm early-stage); RadioPARP hints at concurrent safety
⚠️ Veliparib (TBCRC 024): severe acute + late toxicity with concurrent RT, avoid concurrency
🔍 CDK4/6i concurrency data mostly retrospective; hold for large fields, concurrent only in protocol
studies.significance: The RT-actionable read is agent-specific concurrency, not a single number: continue endocrine therapy, trastuzumab ± pertuzumab, and T-DM1 through breast/CW + nodal RT; hold cytotoxics and veliparib/talazoparib. For T-DXd the ILD lever is dosimetric, not temporal (RT timing 10.7% seq vs 9.6% concurrent, no effect), so sequence/hold hinges on lung dose and baseline pulmonary disease.
studies.monday_clinic: In pts on endocrine therapy, trastuzumab/pertuzumab, or T-DM1 who need breast/CW + nodal RT, this supports continuing concurrently; it does not extend to cytotoxic chemo, capecitabine, or veliparib/talazoparib, which stay sequenced.
open_questions: Concurrent T-DXd with thoracic RT ILD risk in prospective trials
Concurrent CDK4/6 inhibitor with RT safety in large fields
Optimal olaparib to RT sequencing interval in early-stage breast

related_trials.brief_title: Radiation Therapy With or Without Olaparib in Treating Patients With Inflammatory Breast Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial studies how well radiation therapy with or without olaparib works in treating patients with inflammatory breast cancer. Radiation therapy uses high energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Olaparib is an inhibitor of PARP, an enzyme that helps repair deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) when it becomes damaged. Blocking PARP may help keep cancer cells from repairing their damaged DNA, causing them to die. PARP inhibitors are a type of targeted therapy. It is not yet known…

Takeaway Points
• This trial provides evidence that combining node-sparing radiotherapy with
tislelizumab and chemotherapy is an effective treatment strategy for patients
with MMR LARC.
• The experimental arm achieved a remarkable doubling of the pCR rate
(61.0% vs 28.6%, P<0.001), meeting the primary endpoint.
• This innovative approach not only improves tumor response but also reduces
the incidence of severe gastrointestinal adverse events, indicating an
improved tolerability profile.

HER2-Directed Therapy + Radiation
The mAbs are settled - the ADC question is T-DM1 vs T-DXd
13
V Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab - concurrent
with breast/CW + nodal RT is standard practice
Acceptable toxicity, No serious adverse events with concurrent treatment
HERA trial - trastuzumab initiated after completion of chemotherapy and XRT
Finter trial ART was started after compretion of chemotherapy and rastuzumab
NCCTG N9831 - XRT given concurrently with trastuzumab
APHINITY Trial - trastuzumab and pertuzumab given concurrently with RT
T-DM 1 ado-rastuzumab emtansine; t/½ = 4 days, 5t½ = 20 days
CONTINUE during locoregional RT as on
KATHERINE or ATEMPT Trials
KEY TOXICITY SIGNALS
Dermatitis may be underreported (Corbin)
Pneumonitis small signal - maintain vigilance
CNS radionecrosis with SRS - the "do not ignore" signal
USE CAUTION WITH
large lung volumes • IMN coverage • bolus • reconstruction •
CNS SRS
T-DX trastuzumab deruxtecan; t/½ = 6 days, 5t% = 30 days
REASONABLE with caution - monitor closely
DOMINANT TOXICITY - ILD
PI (5.4 mg/kg): ILD ~12%; fatal ~ 0.9%
DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1
RT timing (T-Dd arm): 10.7% seq vs 9.6% concurrent - no effect
COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT; acute tox 20%
SEQUENCE OR HOLD FOR
high lung-dose plans • baseline ILD • thoracic/lung RT •
symptomatic pulmonary disease

Pembrolizumab + RT - from sequential to concurrent
V1 Original - restart pembro ≥ 2 wk post-RT
V2 Amended - concurrent pembro + RT permitted
Post-hoc: 1,174 patients • 715 received RT • concurrent pembro + RT well tolerated;
Numerically fewer G3-5 AEs and Immune AEs in concurrent group vs. sequential group;
Highest rate in Pembre alone and no RT arm (maybe RT is immunosuppressive?)
pneumonitis vigilance maintained
McArthur et al. CancerRes2023;83(5_Supplement)PD3-01
P-RAD (TBCRC-053) - RADIATION AS A BIOLOGIC STRESS TEST
Preoperative RT (0 / 9 / 24 Gy) + pembrolizumab in HR+/HER2- disease
24 Gy + pembro
Statistically significant f in T-cell infiltration at 2
weeks
Tertiary lymphoid structures
Induced
- microenvironment converted toward
immune-active state
Implication
Short-course preop RT can prime the immune
system - foundation for RADAR / ART
2026 ASCO
ANNUAL
MEETING
#ASCO26

CLINICAL
ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS
CANCER

15
PARPi, Capecitabine, and CDK4/6i + Radiation
Where caution is non-negotiable - and where to hold
Olaparib
½ = 15 hr, 5t½ = 3 days
OlympiA, RadioPARP, S1706, OlympiAD
RT must complete 2-12 wks BEFORE olaparib
Sequential paradigm in early-stage;
RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety
Tutt et al., NEJM 2021;384:2394-2405
Loap et al., JROBP, 2025 Nov 1: 123(3):726-731
Veliparib
½ = 5.5 hr, 5t½ = 1 day
TBCRC 024
Severe acute + late toxicity with concurrent RT
AVOID concurrency - dose-limiting moist
desquamation, fibrosis
Jagsi et al., J Clin Oncol 2018; 36(13): 1317-1322
Talazoparib
t½ = 90 hr; 5t½ = 19 days
PK tail
Long t½ complicates brief holds
Favors sequential strategy in early-stage
Capecitabine
t½ = ~45 min; 5t½ = 4 hrs
CREATE-X. Phase I/ Concurrent Trial Woodward et al.
Adjuvant paradigm is sequential, not concurrent
AVOID concurrent breast/CW RT outside
protocol; resume after RT
Masuda et al. N Engl J Med. 2017;376(22):2147-2159.
Woodward et al. JROBP. 2017;99(4):777-783.
Abemaciclib / Ribociclib
t½ = 24-55 hr; 5t = 5-11 days
NCT05996107
Limited prospective data; most evidence
retrospective
Hold during RT for large fields;
concurrent feasible in trial setting
Palbociclib
t½ = 28.8 hr 5t½ = 6 days
5 t½ = 6 days
Marrow / GI exposure increases with field size
Practical to hold around RT; resume
promptly after
When in doubt, hold. PK-based washout → RT → resume — the safer default outside protocols

CLINICAL ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS

16
Putting It Together: A Traffic-Light Summary
Concurrent systemic therapy with breast / chest wall + RNI
V CONTINUE
Concurrent with RT
Endocrine therapy
Minimal radiosensitization
Trastuzumab ‡ Pertuzumab
Generally safe; modern heart-sparing
! CAUTION
Plan- / setting-dependent
T-DXd
Concurrent reasonable with monitoring;
sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active
pulmonary disease
CDK4/6 inhibitors
Usually hold for large fields; concurrent only in
selected/protocol settings
X HOLD / SEQUENCE
Outside dedicated protocol
Cytotoxic chemotherapy
Anthracyclines, taxanes, platinum -
sequence, do not give concurrently
Capecitabine / PARPI
(veliparib/talazoparib)
Adjuvant paradigm non-concurrent, veliparib +
RT shows severe acute/late tox
T-DM1
Continue during locoregional RT; dermatitis &
pneumonitis vigilance; caution with CNS SRS
Immunotherapy (Pembro)
Generally tolerable; pneumonitis vigilance +
immune-toxicity workflows
Olaparib
Reasonable with limited fields; concurrent only
in selected/protocol settings
mTOR / PISK agents
Mucosal, skin, metabolic, inflammatory toxicity
risks; ESMO-ESTRO advises caution
